answer:
We first find the total length of ribosomal protein if stretched out. Each ribosome is 180 Å in diameter, so its volume is \(\frac{4}{3}\pi (90\times10^{-8}\,\text{cm})^3\). With specific gravity 1.0, mass = volume in grams; 40% is protein, so protein mass per ribosome = \(0.4 \times \text{volume}\). Using average amino acid residue length ~3.5 Å and average residue mass ~110 Da, we can compute total polypeptide length for 25,000 ribosomes. The E. coli circumference is \(\pi \times 1\,\mu\text{m} \approx 3.14\,\mu\text{m}\). Doing the calculation yields about \(1.26\times10^4\) times around the cell.

\boxed{B}
